1-1.description: A crystalline wand with a monstrous face.

2-2.description: A faintly glowing trapezohedron.

3-3.description: An jade amulet shaped like a sphinx.

4-4.description: A metallic cluster of globes and half globes.

5-5.description: An oversize, pattern-etched silver key.

6-6.description: A preserved brain in a glass cylinder.

1-1.description: A malevolent curse is transforming the village’s children into misshapen statues.

2-2.description: Guests of a popular local dinner party keep succumbing to autophagia.

3-3.description: Corpses of beasts—mutated and halfway human—are left at the forest’s edge.

4-4.description: Every night, one townsperson melts.

5-5.description: Fiends have infiltrated a community by wearing the corpses of the deceased.

6-6.description: Fleshy catacombs open beneath a town.

7-7.description: A neighborhood is filled with derelict houses composed entirely of mimics.

8-8.description: The corpse of a monstrous leviathan has washed ashore, entrails still moving.

9-9.description: A virulent infestation uses the townsfolk as incubators for giant insects.

10-10.description: Something is stealing the tongues of the city guards.

1-1.description: A vine-covered, ostensibly abandoned prison or asylum.

2-2.description: The cavernous gut of a dead behemoth.

3-3.description: A sprawling university, older than the town surrounding it.

4-4.description: A city of red muscle, with bleeding eyes embedded in hair-strangled buildings.

5-5.description: A forest of burnt pines draped in gore.

6-6.description: A derelict laboratory with old experiments floating in containment tubes.

7-7.description: A slaughterhouse, larger than it appears on the outside and full of victims.

8-8.description: A suddenly empty mortuary.

9-9.description: An old church sitting astride a warren of ancient tunnels.

10-10.description: A vibrant garden sprouting from a mound of putrid corpses.

1-1.description: Possesses a bestial mouth in their torso.

2-2.description: Is pockmarked with eyes that never close.

3-3.description: Grows tooth-filled cysts on their limbs.

4-4.description: Is plagued by an ever-growing fungal mass sprouting from their extremities.

5-5.description: Restitches their body together.

6-6.description: Has parasites writhing beneath their skin.

7-7.description: Can dissolve their physical form at will.

8-8.description: Is gravid with bulbous egg sacs.

9-9.description: Aches with a ceaseless hunger.

10-10.description: Can’t control their transformation into a beast and back.

1-1.description: An abandoned homunculus made in the image of its creator’s child.

2-2.description: A scientist who, hoping to keep their spouse alive, grafts their spouse to their own body.

3-3.description: A fungal mass that grows more intelligent with every sapient life it engulfs.

4-4.description: A guardian angel possessed by the vile blood of the demons it has slain.

5-5.description: A dying ruler obsessed with creating a new body so they can continue their reign.

6-6.description: A cancer possessed by the mind of a dead mage that seeks to regrow their body.

7-7.description: A monarch who feeds their cannibal children, no matter the cost.

8-8.description: The priests of a forgotten god, who try to raise their deity from the faithful’s flesh.

9-9.description: A house that remembers its tenants and will do anything to regain them.

10-10.description: A grieving mortician who sculpts every face they encounter into that of their lost love.

1-1.description: A building converted into a greenhouse.

2-2.description: A hidden caldera or cenote.

3-3.description: An overgrown circle of standing stones.

4-4.description: The sanctuary of a ruined cathedral.

1-1.description: Seeing a particular phase of the moon

2-2.description: Smelling the scent of a certain type of flower

3-3.description: Hearing temple bells ringing

4-4.description: Hearing a particular melody

5-5.description: Touching pure silver with your bare skin

6-6.description: Seeing someone who resembles a specific individual

1-1.description: Has weaponized their blood with poison that will put Strahd to sleep for a generation.

2-2.description: Is hidden from Strahd by another Darklord or some more mysterious evil.

3-3.description: Becomes trapped by a deceitful servant of Strahd, either bodily or by an effect such as a Magic Jar spell.

4-4.description: Knows of their reincarnations and seeks to end them by freeing Barovia from the Mists.

5-5.description: Lives a charmed life, unknowingly protected and cultivated by the count.

6-6.description: Misguidedly plans to redeem Strahd through their exceptional kindness or faith.

7-7.description: Uses magic to hide from Strahd by appearing elderly, childlike, or as a mysterious animal.

8-8.description: Searches for a relic that can weaken Strahd.

9-9.description: Seeks to join a group of adventurers and organize a siege of Castle Ravenloft.

10-10.description: Wants to resurrect past incarnations of Tatyana to battle Strahd.

1-1.description: A child has gone missing in the impossibly vast space beneath their bed.

2-2.description: Something drags a coastal town’s inhabitants out in the night, garbs them in silver, and leads them to the surf to drown.

3-3.description: A wandering minstrel plays music that robs entire cities of sleep.

4-4.description: Local bakers have started making pies filled with a delicious, luminescent, alien, blue goo.

5-5.description: A sapient, unholy tome will do anything to reach a group of cultists.

6-6.description: The inheritors of an ancient sect are transforming into astral horrors.

7-7.description: Animals in the local forest have started going to a specific charred grove to die.

8-8.description: Colossal statues surrounding the city move only during dusk and dawn.

9-9.description: The characters must survive an evening in a sinking ancestral home.

10-10.description: A new wing in the academy’s library has appeared, filled with blank books.

1-1.description: A lavish manor devoid of people—save for staff who swear the lord is busy.

2-2.description: A narrow chapel in the woods, whose bell rings ceaselessly.

3-3.description: A hidden ward of a decrepit university.

4-4.description: A dimension of featureless white, broken up only by staring eyes.

5-5.description: The ninth basement beneath an otherwise normal family home.

6-6.description: A small inn in the mountains that smells perpetually of brine.

7-7.description: An ambulatory forest riddled with carvings of unblinking eyes.

8-8.description: A sewage system that predates its city.

9-9.description: A coastline slowly being consumed by an obsidian sea.

10-10.description: A decrepit lighthouse whose light flickers on at strange intervals.

1-1.description: Is haunted by otherworldly masters that whisper from reflective surfaces.

2-2.description: Sees multiple dimensions at once.

3-3.description: Is transforming into a long-extinct being.

4-4.description: Screams messages from the far-flung future.

5-5.description: Absorbs the words out of books.

6-6.description: Drains any object they focus on of all but one portentous color.

7-7.description: Finds their body warped to match that of the last person they talked with.

8-8.description: Incubates an alien symbiote in their brain.

9-9.description: Sleepwalks, drawing the same symbol on every surface they come across.

10-10.description: Obsesses over music, shifting their form to embody whatever song they hear.

1-1.description: A smiling minstrel whose music drives listeners to murder.

2-2.description: A priest obsessed with creating a shelter safe from the coming apocalypse.

3-3.description: A mayor who will do anything to ensure citizens finish a sacred transformation.

4-4.description: An astronomer broken and enraptured by what they saw in the stars.

5-5.description: A scientist striving to make their body the perfect host for an ageless being.

6-6.description: A coroner who believes the bodies they autopsy contain secret messages.

7-7.description: The head librarian of an ancient sect, who seeks secrets hidden within their peers.

8-8.description: A spoiled noble who intends to raise a cult to feed to residents of an alien dimension.

9-9.description: A researcher knowingly incubating a powerful entity within themself.

10-10.description: A hierophant seeking a doorway to an alien realm to bring about a new world.

1-1.description: Caused them to obsess over control.

2-2.description: Celebrated the Darklord’s heinous activities.

3-3.description: Drove them toward bloodthirsty revenge.

4-4.description: Enabled their indulgence and vices.

5-5.description: Escalated their violent tendencies.

6-6.description: Fueled an all-consuming fear of loss.

7-7.description: Pushed them to turn to a greater power.

8-8.description: Removed their empathy for others.

9-9.description: Sowed paranoia, encouraging them to constantly test their allies’ loyalty.

10-10.description: Spurred them to pursue perfection.

1-1.description: A beholder-shaped statue has come to life and begun terrorizing the city.

2-2.description: A portal to a demonic realm has opened in the mouth of an innocent acolyte.

3-3.description: A sapient virus is turning the inhabitants of a village into shadows.

4-4.description: Giant spiders have declared war on all bipedal creatures.

5-5.description: A coven plans to use a volcano as a massive cauldron for a catastrophic spell.

6-6.description: The nation’s beloved monarch was recently revealed to be a lich.

7-7.description: A malevolent phantom haunts the moon, raising corpses bathed in its light as undead.

8-8.description: A sentient weapon transforms its surroundings into a mockery of the war it last fought in.

9-9.description: A factory distills souls into a powerful wish-granting elixir.

10-10.description: An expanding labyrinth has just swallowed another neighborhood.

1-1.description: The floating and fallen ruins of a magical megastructure.

2-2.description: A blood-soaked swamp.

3-3.description: A tower that pierces a magical ley line, draining its power.

4-4.description: A criminal consortium’s lavish academy.

5-5.description: A forest where every tree grows from the body of a mummified hero.

6-6.description: An empire covered in the watchful religious symbols of an all-seeing deity.

7-7.description: A fortress with seven locked gates that seal off the underworld.

8-8.description: A country devastated by the fallout of weapons used in an age-old war.

9-9.description: A town threatened by an unstoppable, slowly encroaching infection.

10-10.description: A city adrift on a sea full of primeval predators.

1-1.description: Uncontrollably damages everything they care about.

2-2.description: Manufactures catastrophes to distract from their own ineptitude.

3-3.description: Is surrounded by power-hungry spies and saboteurs, fueling the villain’s paranoia.

4-4.description: Seeks the pleasure of lost glories.

5-5.description: Is surrounded by sycophants whose constant praise makes every success feel hollow.

6-6.description: Is plagued by a cursed family legacy.

7-7.description: Is hounded by rivals and baseless mockery.

8-8.description: Must sacrifice those they love to maintain their facade of power.

9-9.description: Is praised by society for a deed they never truly accomplished.

10-10.description: Elicits excessive fear and reverence, resulting in their isolation.

1-1.description: A machine that believes it’s a resurrected tyrant and seeks to rebuild its empire.

2-2.description: The leader of a subterranean people who plots to blot out the sun’s searing light.

3-3.description: A high priest intent on shifting an entire nation into their god’s realm.

4-4.description: A desperate general who unleashes otherworldly war machines.

5-5.description: A massive treant who has allied with raiders and seeks to fell every forest.

6-6.description: A sage who solves an endless war by preventing anyone from dying.

7-7.description: An eternal royal whose immortality relies on the blood of angels.

8-8.description: A criminal kingpin who burns opponents into psychoactive spirit dust.

9-9.description: An ancient dragon whose godlike magic drains the domain of life.

10-10.description: A god who killed all their peers and now rules the mortal realm.

1-1.description: A taboo the Darklord broke.

2-2.description: Someone the Darklord killed.

3-3.description: Someone or something the Darklord loved—and lost.

4-4.description: Esoteric knowledge the Darklord sought but couldn’t obtain.

5-5.description: A tradition sacred to the Darklord’s home.

6-6.description: An act of extreme violence the Darklord committed.

7-7.description: The Darklord’s greatest temptation.

8-8.description: Someone the Darklord betrayed.

9-9.description: The Darklord’s greatest fear.

10-10.description: Evidence of the Darklord’s vilest act.

1-1.description: Claims to personally know Duchess d’Honaire and so deserve special treatment.

2-2.description: Insists they’re investing all their resources in solving a problem they’re clearly ignoring.

3-3.description: Is publicly trapped or wounded but claims to passersby that they’re fine.

4-4.description: Passes off their rat-meat pies and gutter-water wine as food fads.

5-5.description: Poses as an expert, but their advice is ludicrous or dangerous nonsense.

6-6.description: Says their stolen wares come from Chateaufaux.

1-1.description: What sounds, smells, colors, or symbols pervade the domain?

2-2.description:
Where do the Mists appear besides the
domain’s borders? What shapes and sounds haunt the Mists in and near the domain?

3-3.description: What is visible in the sky? Is there a day-and-night cycle? What’s the weather like?

4-4.description: What is the domain culture’s general attitude toward the Darklord?

5-5.description: What does the domain culture fear or consider taboo?

6-6.description: What does the domain culture celebrate?

7-7.description: What is scarce in the domain, and how do the inhabitants compensate for this?

8-8.description: What do buildings in the domain look like? Are they new or old?

9-9.description: What biome is the domain’s environment (e.g., arctic, desert, forest)?

10-10.description: What justifications do the inhabitants use, if any, to pretend nothing is wrong in the domain?

1-1.description: Azalin’s spirit jar or other magical treasure.

2-2.description: The corpse of an ally whose body rose as a zombie and wandered into the Mists.

3-3.description: A Darklord’s treasure held in Avernus.

4-4.description: Details of Azalin’s fate.

5-5.description: Duplicates of Azalin, an ally, or themselves.

6-6.description: A passage to the King’s Tear.

1-1.description: Bestial eyes glaring from the shadows.

2-2.description: Clouds briefly take on frightful shapes.

3-3.description: A door unlatch and open on its own.

4-4.description: A farm animal or pet curse under its breath.

5-5.description: Flowers turn black, then wither.

6-6.description: A shadow or reflection move on its own.

7-7.description: A spectral orb drift through a wall or door.

8-8.description: A tree branch move like a grasping claw.

5-5.description: A @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmGulthiasBlight]{Gulthias Blight} covered in black roses

6-6.description: Kender Nosferatu (see below)

7-7.description: Shadow Highlord (see below)

8-8.description: Azrael Dax (see below)

1-1.description: Activate the Rift Spanner, a device fueled by souls stolen by the night hag Styrix.

2-2.description: Free Cthulhu, allowing it to escape into the multiverse.

3-3.description: Find Azalin Rex and discover how he escaped Darkon.

4-4.description: Journey into the past and prevent Strahd von Zarovich from becoming the first Darklord.

5-5.description: Sever a Darklord’s tie to their domain, freeing the land back into the multiverse.

6-6.description: Use the device called the Apparatus to transport their souls into other bodies.

1-1.description: Anyone entering the city on horseback is imprisoned and sentenced to death.

2-2.description: A cult requests help summoning a demonic force to combat a greater evil.

3-3.description: The townspeople protect a violent hag, whom they call “grandmother.”

4-4.description: After an estate party, the residents adopt the characters, refusing to let them leave.

5-5.description: The characters bear an uncanny resemblance to the founders of a secret village.

6-6.description: Townsfolk disappear for days before reappearing with missing memories.

7-7.description: Strangers hand out wax earplugs to protect others from “the choir.”

8-8.description: Every night, parents let their children follow floating lights into the forest.

9-9.description: Villagers physically can’t stop smiling.

10-10.description: Anyone who stays in the village overnight loses their shadow to the looming woods.

1-1.description: A copse of dense, blackened woods.

2-2.description: A mansion built around a stone circle.

3-3.description: A field where paths grow in corridor-like patterns leading to a ruin at the center.

4-4.description: A telepathic collective that townsfolk join by ingesting a rare fungus.

5-5.description: A hag’s hut that stands atop a growing hill of rotten sweets.

6-6.description: A glacier that residents never leave.

7-7.description: A cavern where the glowing bones of an otherworldly being jut from the walls.

8-8.description: A countryside with stretches of hayfields and colorful barns.

9-9.description: Rival villages engaged in an age-old war.

10-10.description: A deserted chapel that has been burned down and rebuilt a thousand times.

1-1.description: Knows the beliefs are false but keeps up the facade to maintain power.

2-2.description: Adheres to an ancient faith and desperately works to convert nonbelievers.

3-3.description: Seeks to transform their body into a vessel or gate for the subject of their belief.

4-4.description: Now appears as a bloodthirsty amalgamation of various forest creatures.

5-5.description: Uncontrollably utters prophecies.

6-6.description: Is haunted by judgmental ancestral spirits.

7-7.description: Can appear only at dawn and dusk.

8-8.description: Has partially melded into a hallowed tree.

9-9.description: Is being consumed by fungi.

10-10.description: Can no longer commune with their deity, driving them to ever bloodier sacrifices.

1-1.description: A secluded temple’s high priest who needs to find the perfect sacrifice.

2-2.description: An erinyes that appears when youngsters speak a rhyme into a darkened mirror.

3-3.description: A night hag that dwells in the dreams of those who drink a special ergot tea.

4-4.description: A shape-shifter that takes on the appearance of the last person it fed on.

5-5.description: A wicker giant that animates during the new moon, punishing the unwary.

6-6.description: A mayor who behaves in archaic ways so villagers don’t enrage an ancient ghost.

7-7.description: A treant who demands corpses to replace trees cleared by a town’s construction.

8-8.description: A mysterious robed figure who wears a horned mask and leads a silent cult.

9-9.description: A bloodthirsty forest guardian that resembles a deer with two heads.

10-10.description: A shadow that appears only when individuals look away from the woods’ tree line.

Galleries of Suffering.name: Galleries of Suffering
Galleries of Suffering.description: As the characters explore Veneficus, roll on the following table to determine what they find within.

1-1.description: Another @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmArchmage000000]{Archmage} that behaves like Hazlik (see above), or a similarly disguised @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmIncubus0000000]{Incubus}

2-2.description: Artwork that releases a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmBoneDevil00000]{Bone Devil} if touched

3-3.description: Three @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmMage0000000000]{Mages} arguing about who’s greatest

4-4.description: Three @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwWaxwork000000]{Waxworks} (see chapter 5) disguised as mages arguing about who’s Hazlik’s favorite

5-5.description: One @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmStoneGolem0000]{Stone Golem} sculpted to look like Hazlik

6-6.description: Flawed Magic (see below)

1-1.description: Murder victims linger as ghosts until someone solves their murders.

2-2.description: Bloody graffiti keeps appearing on the city’s walls.

3-3.description: A stubborn miser must visit their haunted family home to put their family to rest.

4-4.description: A mob of angry spirits besieges the local temple on the winter solstice each year.

5-5.description: The adventurers must solve the murder of a ghost who can say only three words.

6-6.description: A ship—with the same name as a vessel lost at sea decades prior—arrives at port.

7-7.description: Rumors abound of a ritual allowing a living individual to trade places with the dead.

8-8.description: The ghosts of an actor’s rivals haunt the local theater.

9-9.description: A noble accidentally broke a family urn, unleashing their cruel ancestral spirits.

10-10.description: Every full moon, spirits in a graveyard rise to seek vengeance for a mass crime.

1-1.description: A decrepit mansion where, for years, nobles killed the staff to keep their secrets.

2-2.description: A graveyard city-state where all living residents are grave keepers.

3-3.description: An opulent yet abandoned library, filled with haunted artifacts.

4-4.description: The site of an ancient battle that permanently devastated the land.

5-5.description: A nation where the residents observe grueling rituals to appease the angry dead.

6-6.description: An expansive forest in which a cruel noble once hunted the poor for sport.

7-7.description: A ruined barn where dozens of remarkable animals lost their lives in a fire.

8-8.description: A picturesque cliff where visiting couples often mysteriously fall to their deaths.

9-9.description: An attic in which a hateful spirit has been sealed for decades.

10-10.description: A dilapidated lighthouse overlooking a black-sand beach.

1-1.description: Can’t touch anyone without stealing a precious memory.

2-2.description: Can temporarily release spirits from the villain’s elaborate scars.

3-3.description: Is hounded by spirits that each represent an emotion the villain can no longer feel.

4-4.description: Drains the life from any animal they see.

5-5.description: Has lost the ability to feel physical sensation.

6-6.description: Can be seen only in reflective surfaces.

7-7.description: Is bound within a building; leaving would permanently destroy them.

8-8.description: Turns to ash in sunlight.

9-9.description: Can communicate only through music and writing.

10-10.description: Siphons the color from objects they possess.

1-1.description: A medium who gains power over spirits by feeding victims to them.

2-2.description: The spirit of a long-dead murderer who continues to kill in death.

3-3.description: A ghostly ancestor who refuses to relinquish control of their kin.

4-4.description: A priest who marks the unworthy for sacrifice to the cathedral’s hungry spirits

5-5.description: A phantom rider who sweeps through the village, stealing away the living.

6-6.description: An unbound spirit that repeatedly manifests in victims’ nightmares.

7-7.description: The capricious phantasm of an amoral accident victim who torments the living.

8-8.description: The spirit of a former tyrant who demands sacrifices from their old domain.

9-9.description: A ghost hunter who inflicts hauntings on clients—then charges to remove the spirits.

10-10.description: The spirit of an evil captain who lurks near their wrecked vessel, sabotaging passing ships.

1-1.description: The city’s rulers secretly are warring hags, wererats, and vampires.

2-2.description: A scientist, never seen and known only through their writing, has disappeared.

3-3.description: A repentant immortal requests help in losing centuries of painful memories.

4-4.description: A beloved social figure turns into a murderous living doll every night.

5-5.description: A pair of spirits brings unspeakable tragedy to any romantic couple in town.

6-6.description: A generational curse forces villagers to sacrifice themselves.

7-7.description: Every night, one random guest of an opulent inn chokes on their own blood.

8-8.description: A serial killer impales victims on a certain monument in a cruel mockery of art.

9-9.description: Terrible noises sound from a dilapidated mansion; those who visit are never seen again.

10-10.description: As the result of abhorrent experiments, blood-drained creatures roam the streets.

1-1.description: A lavish garden that siphons life from visitors to maintain its beauty.

2-2.description: A ruined cathedral devoted to transforming a prophesied being.

3-3.description: A laboratory where preserved body parts carry the minds of their former owners.

4-4.description: A forest of eternal night where bloodthirsty creatures live in fragile peace.

5-5.description: A rectory where the stained glass windows hold the trapped souls of the pious.

6-6.description: A city where all who die are cast in plaster and used to adorn the streets.

7-7.description: An island where the inhabitants make sacrifices to avoid eerie transformations.

8-8.description: An artist’s paradise where cruelties are elevated to terrible and beautiful art forms.

9-9.description: A castle in which visitors are transformed into objects or animals.

10-10.description: A ruined fortress haunted by a monstrous yet docile creature.

1-1.description: Inherited unlimited wealth, but all finery they wear turns into ash.

2-2.description: Endlessly cries tears of blood.

3-3.description: Is covered in tattoos, which magically update to illustrate all their sins.

4-4.description: Turns into a lead statue when the sun rises.

5-5.description: Is incomparably beautiful, but locals perceive them as a beast.

6-6.description: Sees evidence of their impending doom everywhere.

7-7.description: Extinguishes any light shone on them.

8-8.description: Can’t taste food.

9-9.description: Curses anyone they dance with.

10-10.description: Craves company but is cursed to forever wear a mask and obscure their identity.

1-1.description: A reclusive noble who uses their vampiric reputation to terrorize their vassals.

2-2.description: A vain socialite who made a bargain with a fell power to retain their youth.

3-3.description: A scientist obsessed with creating the perfect poison, machine, or life-form.

4-4.description: A beloved magnate who abducts commoners to use in beauty rituals.

5-5.description: A celebrity who murders openly but uses their charm to avoid repercussions.

6-6.description: Someone who loves a monstrous creature and does anything to keep it safe.

7-7.description: A wealthy heir who manipulates the ambitious into committing terrible deeds.

8-8.description: A poisoner who seeks to manipulate history through targeted killings.

9-9.description: An artist who concocts terrible accidents to inspire their art.

10-10.description: A monster hunter who accuses those they consider sinful of being monsters.

1-1.description: An ambush by the thieves (see above).

2-2.description: Chakuna, who runs past covered in blood; then four @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmDisplacerBeast]{Displacer Beasts} attack.

3-3.description: Flooding or rock falls. A character must succeed on a [[/check survival 15 format=long]] or 2 hours are added to the journey.

4-4.description: Four @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwSwarmofRaveno]{Swarms of Ravenous Insects}.*

5-5.description: Three @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwPlagueSpreade]{Zombie Plague Spreaders}* or a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwRelentlessSla]{Relentless Slasher}* that survived a previous trial.

6-6.description: A @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmHydra000000000]{Hydra} or two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmShamblingMound]{Shambling Mounds}.

7-7.description: A @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmTyrannosaurusR]{Tyrannosaurus Rex} or two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmGiantCrocodile]{Giant Crocodiles}.

8-8.description: A murdered member of another group. Roll a die; if you roll an even number, the corpse has a @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.equipment24.Item.dmgPotionOfHeali]{Potion of Healing}.

1-1.description: Awakened sheep plotting against a shepherd.

2-2.description: A cheery home that shows signs of slaughter.

3-3.description: Folk carvings of youths dragged off by wolves.

4-4.description: A fountain depicting dancing wolves.

5-5.description: Lupine paw prints in a tavern or theater.

6-6.description: A songbird singing until its heart stops.

Lychgate Encounters.name: Lychgate Encounters
Lychgate Encounters.description: While traveling through Lychgate or between floors of Castle Avernus, roll on the following table to determine what the characters encounter.

1-1.description: Two scavenging @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmArcanaloth0000]{Arcanaloths} (see Monster Manual)

2-2.description: Two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwDullahan00000]{Dullahans} (see chapter 5) and two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmNightmare00000]{Nightmares}

3-3.description: Two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmHauntingRevena]{Haunting Revenants} (see Monster Manual) disguised as rubble

4-4.description: Two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwRelentlessJug]{Relentless Juggernauts} (see chapter 5)

1-1.description: Freeing a living loved one from possession by another ghost.

2-2.description: Hiding a secret that could embarrass or undermine a living loved one.

3-3.description: Liberating another spirit from the dungeons beneath the House on Gryphon Hill.

4-4.description: Locating a spirit that wanders a broad region or who appears only during the new moon.

5-5.description: Restoring the memory of an ancient spirit that has almost lost its sense of self.

6-6.description: Searching a distant haunted site for a spirit it has been separated from.

7-7.description: Sealing the site of an ancient evil or the lair of a creature that torments spirits.

8-8.description: Smuggling a living relative out of Mordent, away from Wilfred’s clutches.

1-1.description: Does the Darklord feel that they have any hope of redemption? Do they want it?

2-2.description: How did the Darklord react when challenged?

3-3.description: What ideal or philosophy governs the Darklord’s greater actions?

4-4.description: What locations and circumstances shaped the Darklord’s adolescence?

5-5.description: What pushed the Darklord to continue committing heinous acts?

6-6.description: What person, place, or object does the Darklord desire most?

7-7.description: What was the first depraved act the Darklord committed?

8-8.description: Whose respect did the Darklord crave?

9-9.description: Who hurt the Darklord?

10-10.description: Who loved the Darklord?

1-1.description: Drinking water from dirty troughs.

2-2.description: Eating one type of food for all meals.

3-3.description: Excessive and unabashed sunbathing.

4-4.description: Forgoing shoes to feel the dirt.

5-5.description: Pausing and listening to unheard sounds.

6-6.description: Whispering to flowers and houseplants.

Pyramid Heights.name: Pyramid Heights
Pyramid Heights.description: When the characters reach the Shrine of Ankhtepot in the upper levels of Pharaoh's Rest, and when they enter the Lesser Tombs, roll on the following table to determine what lurks within.

1-1.description: @UUID[Actor.sqV4iGfnNS9LuL2C]{Encounter:} A @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmDao00000000000]{Dao} (see Monster Manual) compelled to defend the pyramid

2-2.description: @UUID[Actor.7DlTI1X8bIb5zebf]{Encounter:} Three @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmInvisibleStalk]{Invisible Stalkers} that chant curses

3-3.description: @UUID[Actor.u6dvvVDKF3nPSE5D]{Encounter:} Four @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmMummy000000000]{Mummies} and two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmDeathDog000000]{Death Dogs}

4-4.description: @UUID[Actor.pVq75QIGXEZkFxWF]{Encounter:} Two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmMummy000000000]{Mummies} and two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmShieldGuardian]{Shield Guardians}

5-5.description: @UUID[Actor.frAD6OiDccpgphao]{Encounter:} Two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmStoneGolem0000]{Stone Golems} dressed as pharaohs

6-6.description: @UUID[Actor.wifTw56uGPewSJWV]{Encounter:} Four @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmWraith00000000]{Wraiths} made of black sand

Random Domains of Dread.name: Random Domains of Dread
Random Domains of Dread.description:
Domains marked with an asterisk (*) are featured domains detailed earlier in this chapter.

Cards marked with a dagger (†) are also aligned with another Domain of Dread.

1-1.description: You don’t take an action or a Bonus Action, and you use all your movement to move. Roll [[/r 1d4]] for the direction: 1, north; 2, east; 3, south; or 4, west.

2-6.description: You don’t move or take a Bonus Action, and you take the Attack action to make one melee attack against a random creature within reach. If none are within reach, you take no action.

7-8.description: You have the &Reference[Prone] condition, and your turn ends.

1-1.description: An elf desperately offering a swaddled bundle.

2-2.description: Glimpses of skeletal warriors or horses.

3-3.description: Knights in battle or in the midst of a siege.

4-4.description: Peasants being consumed by shadowy flames.

5-5.description: Marching soldiers with dragon-like features.

6-6.description: Solemn elf priests being ambushed by ogres.

1-1.description: As clouds momentarily resemble clawed hands, gaping skulls, or Castle Ravenloft.

2-2.description: As wolves or ravens soundlessly follow the characters and mirror their actions.

3-3.description: By faded carvings or tapestries bearing the von Zarovich crest.

4-4.description: During a tarokka reading, when the Darklord card changes to resemble Strahd.

5-5.description: In a dream where a character becomes charmed or threatened by Strahd.

6-6.description: In a rustic shrine littered with vials of dried blood, snapped splinters, and wolf fangs.

7-7.description: When characters awake to find swarms of roosting bats or bloodless animal corpses.

8-8.description: When spying villagers try to eavesdrop on or steal small things from the characters.

1-1.description: Anna, the keeper of what another Darklord desires—such as Ankhtepot’s ka or Mordenheim’s Unbreakable Heart (both detailed later in this chapter).

2-2.description: Ez d’Avenir (see chapter 5), a monster hunter.

3-3.description: Ireena Kolyana, adopted daughter of Burgomaster Indirovich of the village of Barovia.

4-4.description: Lyssa von Zarovich, a distant relative of Strahd’s, who has become a vampire.

5-5.description: Nasseri and Yasmine, identical twins who serve the god Ezra.

6-6.description: Petra Rilenovich, an artist who paints images of what she calls “dream lives.”

7-7.description: Quentin L’Argent, the dragonborn son of herders living near Argynvostholt.

8-8.description: Renoir Laurent, the son of Chantal Laurent, a woman Strahd abducted years ago.

9-9.description: Vanasia, a member of a far-ranging Vistani band or other group of mist wanderers.

10-10.description: Vasilka, a @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.actors24.Actor.mmFleshGolem0000]{Flesh Golem} or reborn (see chapter 1) who lives in the abbey of Krezk.

1-1.description: An ankh holy to the old gods of Har’Akir.

2-2.description: A blessing granted to those who survive trials within the domain’s endless sandstorms.

3-3.description: A canopic jar containing the pharaoh’s heart hidden within a forgotten tomb.

4-4.description: A hawk or giant eagle that circles the sun.

5-5.description: Manifested in a joyous song sung by Muhar’s people.

6-6.description: Pieces of a jewel hidden within each of the Children of Ankhtepot.

7-7.description: The soul of a character, perhaps one with the Echoing Soul feat (see chapter 1).

8-8.description: The spirit of an innocent healer who resembles the pharaoh as he was in life.

The Death Head is that of ….name: The Death Head is that of …

1-1.description: A bear

2-2.description: A burned corpse

3-3.description: A hag

4-4.description: A Humanoid

5-5.description: A medusa

6-6.description: A mind flayer

7-7.description: A nothic

8-8.description: A shark

9-9.description: A wolf

10-10.description: An elk

The Death’s Head has this Feature ....name: The Death’s Head has this Feature ...

1-1.description: Bloodshot eyes

2-2.description: Crimson drool

3-3.description: Extra eyes

4-4.description: Face paint

5-5.description: An iron mask

6-6.description: A lengthy tongue

7-7.description: Long hair

8-8.description: Sharpened teeth

9-9.description: Wooden teeth

10-10.description: Exposed bone

Track of the Monster.name: Track of the Monster
Track of the Monster.description: Characters who succeed on a [[/check survival 12 format=long]] find and can follow Kartad's tracks to Hope’s Heart. If the characters fail, a survivor points out the path. Roll on or choose results from the following table for three encounters that happen on the way (rerolling duplicates).

1-1.description: @UUID[Actor.9QAMfaWUDijSXUqF]{Encounter:} Six @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmDireWolf000000]{Dire Wolves} pursue a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmHomunculus0000]{Homunculus}. The homunculus flees as soon as possible.

2-2.description: @UUID[Actor.sM7UcDxYhLUKSVFU]{Encounter:} [[/r 1d8]] @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmZombie00000000]{Zombies} animated by mechanical apparatuses stare into the sky. They are Constructs and don’t react, even if harmed.

3-3.description: A @UUID[Actor.YBbj7BUUZ0J7B9HK]{Primeval Owlbear} (see Monster Manual) made of mismatched parts forages for food; it is a Construct. It is Indifferent to the characters.

4-4.description: @UUID[Actor.INIJELZ2xGOGngLS]{Encounter:} A @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmTroll000000000]{Troll} hunts alongside a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmWinterWolf0000]{Winter Wolf}.

21-40.description: After wandering for another 1d6 hours and rolling again on this table.

66-75.description: A mile from where they entered at midnight of the following night.

76-85.description: Inside a structure within the domain they tried to leave, perhaps a cave, crypt, shed, or closet.

86-95.description: After encountering creatures within the Mists. Roll any die. If you roll an even number, a terrified Commoner appears. If you roll an odd number, a Mist Horror (see chapter 5) appears. Roll again on this table.

96-99.description: Where they entered, only to find the domain changed. Perhaps someone the characters knew is gone and no one remembers the person.

100-100.description: On a world on the Material Plane. After 1d6 hours, the Mists rise around them once more and return them to the domain they departed.

1-1.description: Seeks an apprentice or inheritor.

2-2.description: Explores ways to forget the past.

3-3.description: Longs for divine or otherworldly power.

4-4.description: Pursues ways to relieve themself of loneliness.

5-5.description: Relentlessly pursues the reincarnation of a loved one.

6-6.description: Searches for an object or artifact.

7-7.description: Seeks vengeance against a person or ideal.

8-8.description: Struggles to cheat death.

9-9.description: Wants to be a hero—but one who is twisted to the extreme.

10-10.description: Wishes to be beautiful and admired.
